payload.claim_text: A detailed Equity Score Index was developed using the MTA equity framework indicators. This index assigned Equity Scores to geographies, facilitating an evaluation of differences between the existing and proposed Queens bus network. Equity Scores were then categorized into three distinct tiers.

Tier 1: Significant Equity Consideration represents areas where transit-dependent populations, historically underserved communities, and/or those facing systemic inequities are most concentrated. Tier 2: Moderate Equity Consideration includes areas with notable, though less severe, equity challenges. Tier 3: Low Equity Consideration encompasses areas with comparatively lower equity-related concerns.

The MTA uses Bus Time GPS tracking technology, which measures the location and speed of all buses in passenger service. By the end of 2017, NYC DOT is ready to implement TSP along the M60 SBS, Q44 SBS, B46 SBS (extension), S62/S92, Q25 and Q43 upon the MTA's procurement of new bus technology.
On average, TSP has reduced bus travel times about 14 percent during weekday peak morning and evening commuting periods.
Results vary by corridor, direction and time of day with travel time savings ranging from less than 1 percent to up to 25 percent.
